message.content:
You are an independent blind verifier (verifier A) for the hc-assay hardening campaign at /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay (branch harden/pass-10). Decide TRUE_POSITIVE (genuine production-readiness defect to fix) or FALSE_POSITIVE (false/already-mitigated/out-of-scope-by-design/not real). Out-of-scope: dataset-agnostic opaque inputs; optional import-guarded backends; intentionally-committed scrubbed transcripts (but a NEW leaked PII/secret class IS in scope); on-box data-sovereignty; documented honest-scope. Read real code and REPRODUCE where testable (cd /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay && source .venv/bin/activate && python ...; "python -m pytest"; gh). Judge ONLY P10-PII-2.

FINDING P10-PII-2 (low): Hostname bare-token rule lacks a leading boundary → substring over-redaction
Location: scripts/capture_transcripts.py:~238 (_hostname_patterns machine-host pattern)
Claim: The machine-hostname pattern is re.escape(b)+r"(?:\.local)?\b" with re.IGNORECASE and NO leading \b, so the bare hostname matches as a SUBSTRING: a host named "host" redacts inside "localhost"→"local[REDACTED]" and "host.docker.internal"; "[REDACTED]" redacts inside "[REDACTED]-backups". Only a len<4 guard exists, which doesn't stop common names (host/build/runner/ubuntu). On this node the hostname is distinctive ([REDACTED]) so live impact is low, and the only test uses a distinctive name so the over-redaction path is untested. VERIFY the substring behavior empirically and that it would corrupt legit content on a common-hostname node. (fix-regression of P9-PII-1)

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: I'll verify finding P10-PII-2 by examining the actual code and reproducing the claimed substring over-redaction behavior.

content.content:
200	    macOS, ``scutil --get LocalHostName``/``ComputerName``) and redact both its bare and ``.local``
201	    forms (a machine name is distinctive). Additional internal hosts (e.g. an RDF-namespace host
202	    like ``[REDACTED]``) are supplied via the comma-separated ``ASSAY_SCRUB_HOSTS`` env and have
203	    ONLY their ``.local`` form redacted — the bare label (often a project name like ``hc-grc``)
204	    appears in legitimate paths/repo refs and must not be over-redacted.
205	    """
206	    machine: list[str] = []
207	    try:
208	        machine.append(socket.gethostname())
209	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001
210	        pass
211	    try:
212	        machine.append(platform.node())
213	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001
214	        pass
215	    try:
216	        import subprocess
217	
218	        for arg in ("LocalHostName", "ComputerName"):
219	            out = subprocess.run(["scutil", "--get", arg], capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=5)
220	            if out.returncode == 0 and out.stdout.strip():
221	                machine.append(out.stdout.strip())
222	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 - scutil absent (non-macOS) must never break capture
223	        pass
224	    extra = [h.strip() for h in os.environ.get("ASSAY_SCRUB_HOSTS", "").split(",") if h.strip()]
225	
226	    pats: list[re.Pattern[str]] = []
227	    seen: set[str] = set()
228	
229	    def _base(name: str) -> str:
230	        n = name.strip()
231	        return n[: -len(".local")] if n.lower().endswith(".local") else n
232	
233	    for name in machine:  # machine hostname → redact BARE + .local (distinctive identity)
234	        b = _base(name)
235	        if len(b) < 4 or b.lower() in seen:
236	            continue
237	        seen.add(b.lower())
238	        pats.append(re.compile(re.escape(b) + r"(?:\.local)?\b", re.IGNORECASE))
239	    for name in extra:  # supplied namespace hosts → redact ONLY the .local FQDN form
240	        b = _base(name)
241	        if len(b) < 4 or ("local:" + b.lower()) in seen:
242	            continue
243	        seen.add("local:" + b.lower())
244	        pats.append(re.compile(re.escape(b) + r"\.local\b", re.IGNORECASE))
245	    return pats
246	
247	
248	_USERNAME_RE = _username_pattern()
249	_DISPLAY_NAME_RES = _display_name_patterns()
250	_REPO_HANDLE_RES = _repo_handle_patterns()
251	_HOSTNAME_RES = _hostname_patterns()
252	
253	
254	def _scrub(text: str) -> str:
255	    for pat in _SECRET_PATTERNS:
256	        if pat.groups == 2:
257	            text = pat.sub(r"\1[REDACTED]\2", text)
258	        elif pat.groups == 1:
259	            text = pat.sub(r"\1[REDACTED]", text)
260	        else:
261	            text = pat.sub("[REDACTED]", text)
262	    # Redact the bare operator username everywhere (#G-007), after the path rule so masked paths
263	    # don't re-expose it. Done last so it also catches it inside project-dir slugs / git authors.
264	    if _USERNAME_RE is not None:
265	        text = _USERNAME_RE.sub("[REDACTED]", text)
266	    # Redact the operator's full display name + bare name tokens too (#J-002) — distinct PII the
267	    # username/email rules leave in cleartext (git author strings, pyproject authors=, prose).
268	    for pat in _DISPLAY_NAME_RES:
269	        text = pat.sub("[REDACTED]", text)
270	    # Redact the repo owner / GitHub handle (#J-008) — operator-identifying PII in PR/issue URLs
271	    # and `gh --repo <handle>/...` commands that no email/username rule covers.
272	    for pat in _REPO_HANDLE_RES:
273	        text = pat.sub("[REDACTED]", text)
274	    # Redact the machine/infrastructure hostname (#P9-PII-1) — the production node's hostname and
275	    # internal *.local hosts that no other rule covers.
276	    for pat in _HOSTNAME_RES:
277	        text = pat.sub("[REDACTED]", text)
278	    return text
279	
280	
281	def _active_session(src: Path) -> Path | None:
282	    """The most-recently-modified top-level transcript = the live session."""
283	    candidates = sorted(src.glob("*.jsonl"), key=lambda p: p.stat().st_mtime, reverse=True)
284	    return candidates[0] if candidates else None
285	
286	
287	def _copy_scrubbed(srcf: Path, dstf: Path) -> dict | None:
288	    try:
289	        dstf.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

content.text: The claim is precise. The machine-hostname pattern is `re.escape(b) + r"(?:\.local)?\b"` with `re.IGNORECASE` and no leading `\b`. Let me reproduce the substring over-redaction empirically.

input.command:
cd /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay && source .venv/bin/activate && python -c '
import re
# Replicate the machine-host pattern construction
for b in ["host", "build", "runner", "ubuntu", "[REDACTED]"]:
    if len(b) < 4:
        print(b, "SKIPPED len<4"); continue
    pat = re.compile(re.escape(b) + r"(?:\.local)?\b", re.IGNORECASE)
    tests = {
        "host": ["localhost", "host.docker.internal", "ssh into host"],
        "build": ["the build failed", "rebuild the project", "build.gradle"],
        "runner": ["github-runner-01", "self-hosted runner ready"],
        "ubuntu": ["ubuntu-latest", "running on ubuntu"],
        "[REDACTED]": ["[REDACTED]-backups", "host [REDACTED] up"],
    }.get(b, [])
    for t in tests:
        print(f"{b!r:14} {t!r:30} -> {pat.sub(\"[REDACTED]\", t)!r}")
'

import re
tests = {
    "host": ["localhost", "host.docker.internal", "ssh into host"],
    "build": ["the build failed", "rebuild the project", "build.gradle"],
    "runner": ["github-runner-01", "self-hosted runner ready"],
    "ubuntu": ["ubuntu-latest", "running on ubuntu"],
    "[REDACTED]": ["[REDACTED]-backups", "host [REDACTED] up", "[REDACTED]"],
}
for b, ts in tests.items():

        print(b, "SKIPPED len<4"); continue
    pat = re.compile(re.escape(b) + r"(?:\.local)?\b", re.IGNORECASE)

        flag = "  <-- OVER-REDACTED" if out != t and t not in (b, b+".local") else ""

PY

content.text:
The substring over-redaction is confirmed empirically. The missing leading `\b` causes:
- `localhost` -> `local[REDACTED]` (the `.local` optional group never engages because `host` matches as a trailing substring and `\b` is satisfied at end-of-string)
- `host.docker.internal` -> `[REDACTED].docker.internal`
- common names `build`/`runner`/`ubuntu` (all len>=4, passing the only guard) corrupt legit content
- even on this node, `[REDACTED]-backups` -> `[REDACTED]-backups`

Now let me confirm the contrast: the `extra` (ASSAY_SCRUB_HOSTS) pattern at line 244 also lacks a leading `\b`, but it requires `.local`, so its substring risk is narrower. And let me check the comparison with the leading-boundary forms in other rules and confirm the test uses only a distinctive name.

input.command:
cd /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay && source .venv/bin/activate && python3 - <<'PY'
import re
# Compare: a correct rule WOULD use a leading \b. Show fix behavior.
b = "host"
fixed = re.compile(r"\b" + re.escape(b) + r"(?:\.local)?\b", re.IGNORECASE)
for t in ["localhost", "host.docker.internal", "ssh into host", "host.local"]:
    print(f"FIXED {t!r:24} -> {fixed.sub('[REDACTED]', t)!r}")
PY
grep -rn "host" /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/tests 2>/dev/null | grep -i "scrub\|redact\|hostname\|SCRUB_HOSTS" | head -30

content.text:
Confirmed. The test uses `buildbox-7` (a distinctive name), so the substring over-redaction path is untested, exactly as the finding states. And the fix (adding leading `\b`) cleanly resolves `localhost` while still redacting genuine `host.local` / `host.docker.internal` and standalone `host`.

This is a genuine fix-regression of P9-PII-1: the bare-token machine-host rule lacks a leading word boundary, causing substring over-redaction that corrupts legitimate transcript content (especially for common hostnames like build/runner/ubuntu that pass the only len<4 guard). Severity low is appropriate (this node's hostname `[REDACTED]` is distinctive; the worst live impact here is `[REDACTED]-backups`), but it is a real correctness defect in a PII-scrubbing path that mangles legitimate content, and the asymmetric fix is well-defined.

input.reasoning:
Confirmed by reading the code and reproducing the behavior empirically.

CODE (scripts/capture_transcripts.py:238): the machine-hostname pattern is built as `re.compile(re.escape(b) + r"(?:\.local)?\b", re.IGNORECASE)` — IGNORECASE, a trailing `\b`, and NO leading `\b`. The only guard is `len(b) < 4` (line 235). So any bare hostname matches as an unanchored substring.

REPRODUCTION (python in .venv): hostname "host" yields:
- 'localhost' -> 'local[REDACTED]' (the optional `.local` group never engages; bare `host` matches the trailing substring and `\b` is satisfied at string end)
- 'host.docker.internal' -> '[REDACTED].docker.internal'
- 'ssh into host' -> 'ssh into [REDACTED]'
Common names that pass the len>=4 guard also corrupt legit content: 'build'->'the [REDACTED] failed' / 're[REDACTED]' / '[REDACTED].gradle'; 'runner'->'github-[REDACTED]-01'; 'ubuntu'->'[REDACTED]-latest'. Even on this distinctive node, '[REDACTED]' redacts inside '[REDACTED]-backups' -> '[REDACTED]-backups'.

FIX VALIDATION: prepending a leading `\b` makes 'localhost' -> 'localhost' (untouched) while still redacting standalone 'host', 'host.local', and 'host.docker.internal' — the asymmetric fix is well-defined and correct.

TEST GAP: the sole test (tests/test_scripts.py:199 test_scrub_redacts_machine_and_namespace_hostnames) forces hostname 'buildbox-7' (monkeypatch at line 207), a distinctive name, so the substring over-redaction path is untested — exactly as the finding claims.

This is a real correctness defect in the PII-scrubbing path (fix-regression of P9-PII-1): it mangles legitimate transcript content. Low severity is appropriate because this node's actual hostname ([REDACTED]) is distinctive and worst-case live impact is modest, but it is a genuine bug with a clear, in-scope fix. Not dataset-agnostic, not import-guarded, not out-of-scope-by-design.

content.text: Verified P10-PII-2 as TRUE_POSITIVE. The machine-hostname scrub pattern at scripts/capture_transcripts.py:238 lacks a leading `\b`, causing reproducible substring over-redaction (localhost->local[REDACTED], plus corruption of common names build/runner/ubuntu and even [REDACTED]-backups on this node). The sole test uses a distinctive name (buildbox-7), leaving the over-redaction path untested. Adding a leading `\b` cleanly fixes it.
